scores.correctness_notes: All key details match the gold standard: correct method signature, Task attributes (results, status, success, message, return_code, stdout, stderr, log, id), and error types. Extra details like the success condition (status=='completed' and return_code==0) appear correct.
scores.hallucination_notes: task.raise_on_failure() is introduced as an explicit method, but the gold standard indicates juju.run() itself raises TaskError automatically on failure — this method may not exist and potentially contradicts the documented behaviour. Minor inaccuracy, not a full fabrication.
